# GRE Mathematics Subject Test — Scoring Data & Released-Material Verification

**TL;DR**
- The full GR3768 raw→scaled conversion table was located **verbatim** in the current ETS practice book (Copyright © 2024), page 35; both load-bearing rows are **CONFIRMED**: **60→880** and **57–58→860**.
- The prior agent's interpretive figures (Mean 676 / SD 154 / N 7,452; 880→90th, 860→86th, 800→74th, 680→50th) are **CORRECTED**: they match a now-**SUPERSEDED** ETS edition (© 2024, cohort 2019–2023). The current edition (Copyright © 2025, cohort July 2021–June 2024) reports **Mean 680 / SD 161 / N 5,180** and **880→88, 860→84, 800→71, 680→49**.
- GR3768's table is **NOT** the only public conversion table — form-specific tables also exist for GR1768/GR1268, GR0568, GR9768, GR9367 and GR8767. Six distinct 66-question forms plus a 35-question descriptive-booklet mini-test are public (≈431 official items), all available legally and free; treat them all as training-contaminated.

---

## Section A — GR3768 Full Conversion Table (verbatim) + Verdicts

**Source (PRIMARY ETS):** *Practice Book for the GRE® Subject Test in Mathematics*, Form GR3768, **Copyright © 2024 by ETS**. Table on **page 35**, labeled "Score Conversion Table," with the body header "Score Conversions for the GRE Mathematics Practice Test, Form GR3768 — TOTAL SCORE." URL: https://www.ets.org/pdfs/gre/practice-book-math.pdf. Confidence: **High** (extracted from the machine-readable primary PDF).

Reproduced verbatim (two-column layout flattened to a single mapping):

| Total Correct | Scaled | Total Correct | Scaled |
|---|---|---|---|
| 65–66 | 970 | 33 | 580 |
| 64 | 960 | 32 | 570 |
| 63 | 920 | 31 | 560 |
| 62 | 900 | 30 | 550 |
| 61 | 890 | 29 | 540 |
| 60 | 880 | 28 | 530 |
| 59 | 870 | 27 | 520 |
| 57–58 | 860 | 26 | 510 |
| 56 | 850 | 25 | 500 |
| 55 | 840 | 24 | 490 |
| 53–54 | 830 | 23 | 480 |
| 52 | 820 | 22 | 470 |
| 51 | 810 | 21 | 460 |
| 50 | 800 | 20 | 450 |
| 49 | 790 | 19 | 440 |
| 48 | 770 | 18 | 430 |
| 47 | 760 | 17 | 420 |
| 46 | 750 | 16 | 410 |
| 45 | 740 | 15 | 400 |
| 44 | 720 | 14 | 390 |
| 43 | 710 | 13 | 370 |
| 42 | 700 | 12 | 360 |
| 41 | 680 | 11 | 350 |
| 40 | 670 | 10 | 330 |
| 39 | 660 | 9 | 320 |
| 38 | 650 | 8 | 300 |
| 37 | 630 | 7 | 280 |
| 36 | 620 | 6 | 260 |
| 35 | 610 | 5 | 230 |
| 34 | 600 | 0–4 | 200 |

**Verdicts on the load-bearing rows:**
- **60 → 880: CONFIRMED** (PRIMARY ETS, GR3768 practice book, p. 35).
- **57–58 → 860: CONFIRMED** (PRIMARY ETS, GR3768 practice book, p. 35).

Every other prior-agent anchor point is also **CONFIRMED** verbatim: 65–66→970, 64→960, 63→920, 62→900, 61→890, 59→870, 50→800, 40→670, 33→580. Note GR3768 uses **pure number-correct scoring** ("Your score will be determined by the number of questions you answer correctly… Nothing is subtracted") — no guessing penalty, unlike pre-2012 forms which used Total Score = C − I/4.

---

## Section B — Interpretive Data Verification + Verdicts

**Current PRIMARY source:** *GRE® Subject Test Interpretative Data — Subject Test Total Score Information*, Table 2A (Performance Statistics) and Table 2B (Total Score percentile ranks), **Copyright © 2025 by ETS**. URL: https://www.ets.org/pdfs/gre/gre-guide-table-2.pdf. Confidence: **High**.

**Current Table 2A** (cohort July 1, 2021 – June 30, 2024): *"Mathematics Test 5,180 680 161"* → **N = 5,180, Mean = 680, SD = 161.**

**Current Table 2B percentiles (Mathematics column, "percent scoring lower than selected scaled score"):** 980→94, 960→95, 940→94, 920→93, 900→91, **880→88**, **860→84**, 840→79, 820→75, **800→71**, 780→68, 760→64, 740→61, 720→56, 700→53, **680→49**, 660→46, 640→41, 620→38, 600→34, 580→30, 560→25, 540→22, 520→18, 500→14, 480→11, 460→8, 440→6, 420→4, 400→3.

**Provenance of the prior agent's numbers:** The figures **Mean 676 / SD 154 / N 7,452** and **880→90, 860→86, 800→74, 680→50** are real ETS values, but from the **immediately prior edition** — *GRE Subject Test Interpretive Data*, **Copyright © 2024**, cohort **July 1, 2019 – June 30, 2023** (the same 2019–2023 cohort also appears in the © 2024 *Interpreting Your GRE Scores* booklet, "Table 3," which lists "Mathematics 7,452 676 154"). That edition has been **SUPERSEDED** by the © 2025 release.

**Verdicts:**
- Mean 676 / SD 154 / N 7,452: **CORRECTED** → current = **680 / 161 / 5,180**.
- Percentiles 880→90 / 860→86 / 800→74 / 680→50: **CORRECTED** → current = **880→88 / 860→84 / 800→71 / 680→49**.
- ETS states this interpretive data is **"updated annually"**; treat any single edition as a dated snapshot. The exact ETS document title is *"GRE® Subject Test Interpretative Data — Subject Test Total Score Information,"* table labels **Table 2A** (summary statistics) and **Table 2B** ("GRE Subject Test Total Score Interpretive Data Used on Score Reports").

**Weak-spot flag (document inconsistency):** In the live current PDF, **Table 2A is headed "July 1, 2021, and June 30, 2024"** with a "Copyright © 2025" line, but the **Table 2B sub-header still reads "July 1, 2019, and June 30, 2023."** The surrounding text and copyright indicate the intended current cohort is 2021–2024; the 2B date line appears to be a residual editing artifact in the ETS file. Flag this if precision on the percentile cohort window matters — the percentile values themselves (880→88, etc.) differ from the prior 2019–2023 set, so they reflect the newer data despite the stale header.

---

## Section C — Enumerated Official Released Forms / Material

The subagent verified directly from primary/archival PDFs that **every full-length ETS Math Subject Test booklet carries its own form-specific raw→scaled conversion table** (one scaled score per raw score for that edition), in addition — for the newer booklets — to a generic "Form A/B/C" anchor table.

| Form ID | Year / copyright | Has its own raw→scaled conversion table? | Form ceiling | Where available (legal) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **GR3768** | Current free book, © 2024 | **Yes** — full per-raw table, p. 35 | 970 (raw 65–66) | Free at ets.org (`practice-book-math.pdf`) |
| **GR1768** (= GR1268) | Prior free book (2010s) | **Yes** — "Score Conversions…Form GR1768"; raw 63–66→910 | 910 | Free ETS rehosts; questions identical to GR1268 |
| **GR1268** | ~2004–2008 era | **Yes** (same questions/table family as GR1768) | 910 | University / prep rehosts (Rutgers, Rambo) |
| **GR0568** | Test © 1999/2000/2003/2005; book © 2008 | **Yes** — "Score Conversions and Percents Below…Form GR0568"; raw 51→800 | 900 (raw 65–66) | Rutgers/Rambo PDFs |
| **GR9768** (= GR9767) | "Rescaled," © 2001 | **Yes** — "…Form GR9768"; raw 66→890, 55→800 | 890 | Was the free ETS book pre-2008; rehosts (WOU, Rutgers) |
| **GR9367** | Early/mid-1990s | **Yes** — form-specific; raw 50–66→990, 33→800 | 990 | ETS *Practicing to Take the Mathematics Test* (2nd/3rd ed.) |
| **GR8767** | © 1986 | **Yes** — "…Form GR8767 ONLY"; raw 60–66→990, 40→800 | 990 | ETS *Practicing to Take the Mathematics Test* (1st ed.); rehosts |
| **"Descriptive Booklet 97-99"** (labeled GRDB67 by mathsub) | ETS, 1997–99 | Carries (now outdated) scaled/percentile info, not a full per-raw table | — | Rutgers rehost (`math97-99.pdf`); 35-question mini-test |

Distinct **full-length 66-question forms = six** (GR3768; GR1268/GR1768 counted once; GR0568; GR9768/GR9767 counted once; GR9367; GR8767). The current computer-delivered test (online since the September 2023 administration; still ≈66 questions, 2 h 50 min) does **not** publish a separate practice form beyond the GR3768 practice book.

**Answer to "is any conversion table other than GR3768's public?"** — **Yes, multiple.** Form-specific public tables exist for GR1768/GR1268 (ceiling 910), GR0568 (900), GR9768 (890), GR9367 (990) and GR8767 (990). GR3768 is simply the **only one in the current free ETS booklet**. (The newer booklets GR9768 and GR0568 also include a generic "Range of Raw Scores Needed to Earn Selected Scaled Score on Three Mathematics Test Editions — Form A/B/C" table showing only 800/700/600/500 anchor rows; the oldest GR8767/GR9367 booklets contain only the form-specific table.)

---

## Section D — Total Official-Item Inventory + Leakage Implications

- **Item count:** six distinct full forms × 66 = **396**, plus the **35**-question Descriptive Booklet = **≈431 official released items**. (GR1268 and GR1768 are the same test and are counted once; GR9767/GR9768 likewise.)
- **Legal access:** GR3768 is free at ets.org. The older forms are free ETS materials archived at university math departments (Rutgers/Ian Coley, Western Oregon University) and tutoring sites (Rambo, mathsub); the two oldest (GR8767, GR9367) originally shipped in the out-of-print ETS book *Practicing to Take the Mathematics Test* and circulate as scanned PDFs.
- **Leakage implication (critical for your validity check):** All ≈431 items are long-standing public PDFs and are therefore very likely present in any large web-scraped training corpus. **Any released ETS item used as a benchmark question would be compromised.** For a leakage-free internal assessment, **author original items**; do not reuse released ETS questions. Treat the entire ≈431-item corpus (all six forms + the mini-test) as "known-contaminated" and exclude it from any held-out evaluation set.

---

## Section E — Could-Not-Verify / Explicit Caveats

- **GR8767 and GR9367 per-row conversion values: PARTIALLY VERIFIED.** The original Rutgers scans of these two oldest forms are image-only (non-machine-readable). Their verbatim per-row digits were recovered from third-party rehosts (a Gonzaga hosting of GR8767 and a dokumen.tips reproduction of the *Practicing to Take the Mathematics Test* booklet) — **SECONDARY**. Their form-specific nature, 990 ceilings, and formula scoring (C − I/4) are corroborated, but the exact intermediate row digits **could not be verified against a fully machine-readable primary ETS file**. Use with that caveat.
- **mathsub.com "2019 Unofficial Raw Score Conversion Chart": SECONDARY / unofficial.** It is explicitly built from linear regression and "educated guesses," not ETS data. Do **not** cite its "modern" column as official. Its only role here is to corroborate that each listed form shipped with its own official table.
- **The "200–990" scale is nominal, not achievable on a single form.** In practice each form's reported ceiling is set by equating: GR3768 = 970, GR1768/GR1268 = 910, GR0568 = 900, GR9768 = 890; the pre-2001 (pre-rescaling) GR8767/GR9367 reached 990 precisely because too many test-takers were maxing out — which is why ETS rescaled in October 2001 and warns that pre- and post-2001 scores are not comparable. Wikipedia's claim that "recent" maxima are 920 is a third-party generalization tied to an older cohort and should not be treated as a current ETS statement.
- **Scale-ceiling verdict:** **CONFIRMED** that reported Math scores top out below 990 on modern forms (GR3768 max = 970), and the reason is the **form-specific equating ceiling**, not a change in the nominal 200–990 scale.

---

## Recommendations

1. **Use the GR3768 table (Section A) as your only current, fully primary-verified Math conversion.** Display its ceiling as **970**, not 990, for that form. This is the single most defensible per-form table you can publish today.
2. **Replace every interpretive figure with the © 2025 values:** Mean 680, SD 161, N 5,180; percentiles 880→88 / 860→84 / 800→71 / 680→49. Add a visible note: "ETS updates Subject Test interpretive data annually; figures reflect the July 2021–June 2024 cohort." **Re-check the ETS PDF each year**, because the prior agent's numbers became stale within one cycle.
3. **For the topic-coverage map and any scored test, author original items.** Treat all ≈431 released ETS items as training-contaminated and exclude them from held-out evaluation. Benchmark threshold to revisit this: only if ETS releases a *new* sealed form not yet on the public web could you consider reusing official items — and even then, verify it is absent from crawl indices first.
4. **If you display a projected 200–990 range, label it "nominal scale; achievable ceiling is form-dependent (e.g., 970 on GR3768)."** Do not imply a test-taker can reach 990 on a current form.
5. **Decision trigger:** if a future ETS interpretive-data edition shows Mean/SD shifting materially (e.g., >1 SD-equivalent) or N changing sharply, re-baseline any percentile-driven UI before launch.

---

## Sources

**Primary (ETS):**
- *Practice Book for the GRE® Subject Test in Mathematics*, Form **GR3768**, Copyright © 2024 by ETS — full test, answer key/P+ worksheet (p. 34), and Score Conversion Table (p. 35). https://www.ets.org/pdfs/gre/practice-book-math.pdf
- *GRE® Subject Test Interpretative Data — Subject Test Total Score Information*, Tables 2A & 2B, **Copyright © 2025 by ETS** (cohort July 2021–June 2024). https://www.ets.org/pdfs/gre/gre-guide-table-2.pdf
- *GRE® Subject Test Interpretive Data*, **© 2024 edition** (cohort July 2019–June 2023; Mathematics N 7,452 / Mean 676 / SD 154) — superseded; located via ETS-India mirror. https://www.ets.org/content/dam/ets-india/pdfs/gre/gre-guide-table-2.pdf
- *Interpreting Your GRE® Scores: 2024 and 2025–26*, ETS — Subject Test "Table 3" (2019–2023 cohort figures). https://www.ets.org/pdfs/gre/interpreting-gre-scores.pdf
- ETS GRE Subject Test pages: "Understanding Your GRE Subject Test Scores" (https://www.ets.org/gre/test-takers/subject-tests/scores/understand-scores.html); "GRE Subject Test Content and Structure" (https://www.ets.org/gre/test-takers/subject-tests/about/content-structure.html); "GRE Subject Tests" / Guide to the Use of Scores landing (https://www.ets.org/gre/score-users/about/subject-tests.html); "GRE Subject Test to be Computer Delivered" (https://www.ets.org/gre/score-users/subject-test-changes.html).

**Primary (older ETS forms, via university/archival rehosts):**
- **GR0568** practice book, © 1999/2000/2003/2005 (book © 2008) — form-specific conversion table. https://sites.math.rutgers.edu/~iacoley/gre/Practice%202.pdf
- **GR9768** ("Mathematics Test (Rescaled)"), © 2001 — "Score Conversions and Percents Below…Form GR9768." https://cdn.wou.edu/math/files/2015/05/MathGRE.pdf and https://sites.math.rutgers.edu/~iacoley/gre/Practice%205.pdf
- **GR1768** conversion table (raw 63–66→910), ETS practice book p. 63 (Course Hero rehost of the ETS page). https://www.coursehero.com/file/24833179/practice-book-math63pdf/
- **GR8767** (© 1986) and **GR9367** (early/mid-1990s) — form-specific tables; image-only Rutgers scans (Practice 4 / Practice 3) corroborated by third-party machine-readable rehosts. *Per-row digits SECONDARY.*

**Secondary (corroboration / labeling only):**
- mathsub.com — "Resources" (form list, rescaling history) and the **unofficial** "2019 Raw Score Conversion Chart" (https://www.mathsub.com/resources/; https://www.mathsub.com/wp-content/uploads/2019-mathsub-conversion-chart.pdf).
- Rambo Tutoring "Math GRE" page listing the public forms GR1768/GR1268/GR0568/GR8767/GR9367/GR9768 (http://rambotutoring.com/math-gre/).
- mathematicsgre.com forum threads enumerating available papers, incl. GR3768 (https://mathematicsgre.com/viewtopic.php?t=6138; t=234; t=11).
- Wikipedia, "GRE Mathematics Test" (background on rescaling and paper→computer move; its specific mean/max figures are dated and not used for the verified numbers above).
